caption: The day I brought it home. Replica TE37s, replica Mugen wing, faded clearcoat. This is the starting point.
alt: 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in faded Milano Red parked in driveway, rear three-quarter view, with bronze replica TE37 wheels and a black replica Mugen rear wing.

caption: 2021. Replica wheels, replica wing, factory paint. I was figuring out what the car would become.
alt: Rear three-quarter view of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in Milano Red parked in driveway, with bronze replica TE37 wheels and black replica Mugen rear spoiler.

caption: Original rear bumper, dealer plate still on. The wing is a replica Mugen — stayed on for a while before priorities sorted themselves out.
alt: Rear view of stock 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in original Milano Red, showing factory bumper with scuffs, dealer license plate frame, and a black replica Mugen rear spoiler.

caption: B18C1 as I bought it. Stock top end, basic cone filter, factory bay. Compare to where it ended up.
alt: Stock B18C1 engine bay of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, with original red Honda VTEC valve cover, factory wiring, basic aftermarket red cone air filter, and unrestored Milano Red engine bay paint.

caption: Original Acura wheel and cluster. Both replaced later — JDM wheel reupholstered with red stitching, ID4Motion digital cluster behind it.
alt: Stock interior of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R showing factory black Acura airbag steering wheel, analog gauge cluster, dashboard, and shift boot with red stitching.

caption: Original GS-R leather rear seats, torn at the seam. Reupholstered later with red stitching to match the fronts.
alt: Original black leather rear bench seat of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, showing wear and a torn seam on the driver's side cushion.

caption: Empty trunk, factory carpet, OEM parcel shelf. Exposed wiring from a previous owner's stereo install — pulled out later when the car went glass-out for paint.
alt: Open trunk of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R viewed from the rear, showing factory black carpet, OEM parcel shelf, and loose wiring from a previous stereo installation, with rear seats and dashboard visible through the cabin.

caption: Door cards off. The factory water-shield foil and original wiring exactly as Honda put it together in 1995.
alt: Driver-side door of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with the interior door panel removed, showing the metal door frame, OEM water-shield foil, factory wiring harness, lock and window mechanisms, and an aftermarket speaker.

caption: B18C1 coming apart. Crank and rotating assembly still in, everything else stripped.
alt: Partially disassembled Honda B18C1 engine on a stand, showing the bare aluminum block with crankshaft and main bearing caps still installed, surrounded by tools and parts on a garage floor.

caption: Bare block. Sent to CSS for machine work after this — overbore for the Traum 81.5mm forged pistons.
alt: Bare Honda B18C1 engine block with all four cylinder bores exposed and crankcase visible, photographed from above on a workbench, with the B18C1 casting code stamped on the side.

caption: Front fender off. Easier access to the engine bay and a clean look at the inner structure before bodywork.
alt: Front passenger-side wheel well of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with the outer fender removed, exposing the inner fender liner, suspension components, and engine bay wiring.

caption: GS-R fronts pulled. Worn but the bones were good — sent out to be reupholstered with red stitching.
alt: Original black leather front seats from a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, removed from the car and placed side by side on a garage floor, showing visible wear, creasing, and a tear on the passenger seat.

caption: Rear bench out. Same trip to the upholsterer as the fronts.
alt: Original black leather rear bench seat of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, removed from the car and placed on a concrete floor, showing wear and a torn cushion seam.

caption: Shifter rod boot, cracked clean through. Replaced — small part, but this is what 30 years looks like.
alt: Close-up of a cracked rubber shifter linkage boot at the floor pan of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, with the metal shift rod passing through the deteriorated grommet.

caption: Side moldings shaved, body filler feathered out. Deleting every factory line that didn't belong before color goes on.
alt: Driver-side rear quarter of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R during body prep, with side moldings removed, body filler smoothed across the panel, and masking tape protecting the rear glass.

caption: Engine bay masked, cowl pulled, every opening foiled. Glass-out, panels off — bay gets the same R-81 as the body.
alt: Engine bay of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R fully prepped for paint, with the front end stripped, masking and aluminum foil protecting openings, and a person reaching in to finalize prep.

caption: First Molding carbon kevlar hood, vents masked, ready for primer. The everyday hood gets prepped first.
alt: First Molding carbon kevlar hood for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R on a paint booth stand, sanded to a uniform matte finish with vents masked off in yellow tape.

caption: OEM hood underside, primed and sanded. Honda paid attention to what you don't see — figured I would too.
alt: Underside of OEM hood for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R primed flat black on a paint booth stand, with masking tape across the inner reinforcement.

caption: Mockup. Quad headlight conversion fitted, OEM hood gapped, fenders blocked. Checking lines before paint.
alt: Front three-quarter view of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R during body prep, with the 98-spec quad headlight conversion test-fitted, OEM hood in primer, and the engine bay still in factory paint.

caption: Front quarter blocked and feathered. Fresh body filler next to original Milano Red — you can see how much red has changed in 30 years.
alt: Close-up of driver-side front fender on 1995 Acura Integra GS-R, showing primer and body filler feathered into faded original Milano Red paint, with a temporary registration permit visible through the windshield.

caption: Two days a week between his shop and my garage for four years. Built it together.
alt: Mechanic working under the front end of a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R during body prep, with the engine bay stripped, hood removed, masking and aluminum foil protecting the windshield and openings.

caption: I bought a whole rear hatch from a junkyard just so my glass guy could carefully cut the back window out with the weather strip intact.
alt: Rear hatch glass being lifted off a 1995 Acura Integra GS-R during glass-out preparation for paint, with two people supporting the glass and the body shell exposed below.

caption: Glass off, hatch frame exposed. Original weather strip preserved on the donor — that was the whole point.
alt: Rear three-quarter view of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with rear hatch glass removed, showing the exposed hatch frame in primer and faded factory paint, with the trunk lid in fresh white primer above.

caption: Type T rear valance and OEM bumper, primed and ready. Subtle from the side, sharper from behind.
alt: Type T rear valance and OEM rear bumper for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in white primer, stacked together inside a paint booth.

caption: Type T side skirts in primer, drying. Last of the JDM body kit before final paint match.
alt: Pair of Type T side skirts for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R hanging on paint stands in white primer, ready for final paint application.

caption: Driver fender, JDM side marker hole cut. USDM cars didn't have them — JDM did, so I added them.
alt: Driver-side front fender of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in gray primer test-fitted to the body, with the original Milano Red paint visible behind and a blue Honda VTEC valve cover in the engine bay.

caption: 98-spec front bumper test-fit. Verifying the conversion lines up with the new fender and hood gaps.
alt: Front three-quarter view of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with 98-spec front bumper conversion test-fitted in satin black, quad headlights installed, and the driver fender in primer alongside.

caption: Headlight and turn signal alignment checked. Quad conversion has to sit right or the whole face looks off.
alt: Close-up of 98-spec front bumper conversion on 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in satin black, showing both quad headlights, the embossed Integra script, and the amber turn signal.

caption: Old bumper off. Stripping the front end down to test-fit the 98-spec.
alt: Front of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with bumper removed for fitment, showing the radiator support, intercooler, and original headlight assemblies on either side, with the blue Honda VTEC valve cover visible above.

caption: Passenger fender, same treatment. Side marker hole cut, fender block-sanded smooth.
alt: Passenger-side front fender of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in gray primer test-fitted to the body, with the original Milano Red paint behind and a yellow microfiber towel in the engine bay.

caption: OEM rear bumper test-fit. Marker holes circled in yellow — verifying panel lines before paint.
alt: OEM rear bumper of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in silver primer test-fitted to the rear of the car, with marker delete locations circled in yellow paint pen and the trunk opening exposed above.

caption: Straight-on test fit. Checking the bumper sits flush across the body line and the trunk gap.
alt: Rear view of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with OEM rear bumper test-fitted in silver primer, showing the license plate bracket, embossed Integra script, and exhaust cutout below the trunk lid in white primer.

caption: Driver-corner test fit. Confirming the bumper meets the quarter cleanly before color.
alt: Driver-side rear corner of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with OEM rear bumper test-fitted in silver primer, showing the body line gap, exposed taillight opening above, and a person's foot in frame at left.

caption: Body shell in the booth. R-81 going on, glass-out, every panel exposed.
alt: Body shell of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R in a paint booth being sprayed Milano Red R-81, with masking plastic still in place over the windows and openings.

caption: First time in the sun. Fresh R-81 against bare metal where the side marker still needs to go.
alt: 1995 Acura Integra GS-R outdoors in fresh Milano Red R-81 paint, with bare metal still visible at the side marker location on the front fender.

caption: Door jambs and B-pillar painted. Inside gets the same color as outside.
alt: Interior door jamb and B-pillar of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81, with masking still in place.

caption: Engine bay sprayed R-81. Firewall to fenders, all one color.
alt: Engine bay of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81 from firewall to inner fenders, with foil and tape protecting masked components.

caption: Every painted piece laid out. Front bumper, GS-R side skirts, Type R spoiler, mirror caps, headlight bezels, door handles — all R-81.
alt: All painted exterior pieces of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R laid out together in fresh Milano Red R-81, including the front bumper, GS-R side skirts, Type R rear spoiler, mirror caps, headlight bezels, and door handles.

caption: Doors, fenders, hood. All painted, all drying.
alt: Both doors, both fenders, and the hood of 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81, drying inside the paint booth.

caption: 98-spec front bumper, fresh R-81. The face of the car.
alt: 98-spec front bumper for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81, drying on a stand.

caption: First Molding carbon kevlar hood. R-81 over the panel, carbon weave masked through the vents.
alt: First Molding carbon kevlar hood for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81, with the carbon kevlar weave still visible through masked vents.

caption: OEM hood underside, fresh R-81. Honda detailed what you don't see — figured I would too.
alt: Underside of OEM hood for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R freshly painted Milano Red R-81.

caption: OEM hood, fresh paint flowing. The hood I save for special occasions.
alt: OEM hood for 1995 Acura Integra GS-R with wet Milano Red R-81 paint freshly sprayed and flowing across the top surface.
